tests.test_body:
define void @pr142286() {
start:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop3, %start
  %phi = phi i8 [ -1, %start ], [ 0, %loop3 ]
  br label %loop2

loop2:                                            ; preds = %loop3, %loop
  br label %loop3

loop3:                                            ; preds = %loop3, %loop2
  switch i8 %phi, label %exit [
    i8 0, label %loop3
    i8 1, label %loop2
    i8 2, label %loop
  ]

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop3
  ret void
}

issue.title: CorrelatedValuePropagation miscompilation
issue.body:
Like https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/139866 this is from a fuzzer that generates Rust MIR: https://github.com/cbeuw/rustlantis.

The Rust MIR reproducer is: https://godbolt.org/z/sb568G8r4

With this command line, the program runs:
```
rustc 9870897538617236803.rs -Copt-level=2 -Cllvm-args=-opt-bisect-limit=478
```
Any greater value of opt-bisect-limit runs CorrelatedValuePropagation on `main` and produces a program that just executes a `ud2`.
issue.author: saethlin

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
https://llvm.godbolt.org/z/s1sYaWbc3
```llvm
; RUN: opt -S -passes=correlated-propagation < %s
define void @test() {
start:
  br label %loop

loop:
  %phi = phi i8 [ -69, %start ], [ 0, %loop3 ]
  br label %loop2

loop2:
  br label %loop3

loop3:
  switch i8 %phi, label %exit [
    i8 0, label %loop3
    i8 1, label %loop2
    i8 2, label %loop
  ]

exit:
  call void @foo()
  ret void
}

declare void @foo()
```

history.commit_subject: Reland "[CVP] Check whether the default case is reachable (#79993)" (#96089)
history.commit_message:
Reland "[CVP] Check whether the default case is reachable (#79993)" (#96089)

This patch reverts https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/81585 as
https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/78582 has been landed.
Now clang works well with reproducer
https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/79993#issuecomment-1936822679.

tests.test_body:
define i8 @switch_defaultdest_multipleuse(i8 %t0) {
entry:
  %o = or i8 %t0, 1
  %r = srem i8 1, %o
  switch i8 %r, label %exit [
    i8 0, label %exit
    i8 1, label %exit
  ]

exit:                                             ; preds = %entry, %entry, %entry
  ret i8 0
}

define i32 @switch_range(i32 %cond) {
entry:
  %s = urem i32 %cond, 3
  %s1 = add i32 %s, 1
  switch i32 %s1, label %unreachable [
    i32 1, label %exit1
    i32 2, label %exit2
    i32 3, label %exit1
  ]

exit1:                                            ; preds = %entry, %entry
  ret i32 1

exit2:                                            ; preds = %entry
  ret i32 2

unreachable:                                      ; preds = %entry
  ret i32 0
}

define i32 @test_default_is_already_unreachable(i32 %num) {
entry:
  %sub = add i32 %num, -120
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %sub, 3
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %sub, i32 2
  switch i32 %cond, label %sw.default [
    i32 0, label %sw.bb
    i32 1, label %sw.bb2
    i32 2, label %sw.bb4
  ]

sw.bb:                                            ; preds = %entry
  %call = call i32 @call0()
  br label %cleanup

sw.bb2:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %call3 = call i32 @call1()
  br label %cleanup

sw.bb4:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %call5 = call i32 @call2()
  br label %cleanup

sw.default:                                       ; preds = %entry
  unreachable

cleanup:                                          ; preds = %sw.bb4, %sw.bb2, %sw.bb
  %retval.0 = phi i32 [ %call5, %sw.bb4 ], [ %call3, %sw.bb2 ], [ %call, %sw.bb ]
  ret i32 %retval.0
}

declare i32 @call0()

declare i32 @call1()

declare i32 @call2()

define i32 @test_unreachable_default_shared_edge(i32 noundef %num) {
entry:
  %sub = add i32 %num, -120
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %sub, 3
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %sub, i32 2
  switch i32 %cond, label %sw.bb4 [
    i32 0, label %sw.bb
    i32 1, label %sw.bb2
    i32 2, label %sw.bb4
  ]

sw.bb:                                            ; preds = %entry
  %call = call i32 @call0()
  br label %cleanup

sw.bb2:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %call3 = call i32 @call1()
  br label %cleanup

sw.bb4:                                           ; preds = %entry, %entry
  %val = phi i32 [ %sub, %entry ], [ %sub, %entry ]
  %call5 = call i32 @call4(i32 %val)
  br label %cleanup

cleanup:                                          ; preds = %sw.bb4, %sw.bb2, %sw.bb
  %retval.0 = phi i32 [ %call5, %sw.bb4 ], [ %call3, %sw.bb2 ], [ %call, %sw.bb ]
  ret i32 %retval.0
}

declare i32 @call0()

declare i32 @call1()

declare i32 @call4(i32)

define i32 @test_unreachable_default(i32 noundef %num) {
entry:
  %sub = add i32 %num, -120
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %sub, 3
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %sub, i32 2
  switch i32 %cond, label %sw.default [
    i32 0, label %sw.bb
    i32 1, label %sw.bb2
    i32 2, label %sw.bb4
  ]

sw.bb:                                            ; preds = %entry
  %call = call i32 @call0()
  br label %cleanup

sw.bb2:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %call3 = call i32 @call1()
  br label %cleanup

sw.bb4:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %call5 = call i32 @call2()
  br label %cleanup

sw.default:                                       ; preds = %entry
  %call6 = call i32 @call3()
  br label %cleanup

cleanup:                                          ; preds = %sw.default, %sw.bb4, %sw.bb2, %sw.bb
  %retval.0 = phi i32 [ %call6, %sw.default ], [ %call5, %sw.bb4 ], [ %call3, %sw.bb2 ], [ %call, %sw.bb ]
  ret i32 %retval.0
}

declare i32 @call0()

declare i32 @call1()

declare i32 @call2()

declare i32 @call3()

define i32 @test_unreachable_default_cond_may_be_undef(i32 %num) {
entry:
  %sub = add i32 %num, -120
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %sub, 3
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %sub, i32 2
  switch i32 %cond, label %sw.default [
    i32 0, label %sw.bb
    i32 1, label %sw.bb2
    i32 2, label %sw.bb4
  ]

sw.bb:                                            ; preds = %entry
  %call = call i32 @call0()
  br label %cleanup

sw.bb2:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %call3 = call i32 @call1()
  br label %cleanup

sw.bb4:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %call5 = call i32 @call2()
  br label %cleanup

sw.default:                                       ; preds = %entry
  %call6 = call i32 @call3()
  br label %cleanup

cleanup:                                          ; preds = %sw.default, %sw.bb4, %sw.bb2, %sw.bb
  %retval.0 = phi i32 [ %call6, %sw.default ], [ %call5, %sw.bb4 ], [ %call3, %sw.bb2 ], [ %call, %sw.bb ]
  ret i32 %retval.0
}

declare i32 @call0()

declare i32 @call1()

declare i32 @call2()

declare i32 @call3()

define i32 @test_reachable_default(i32 noundef %num) {
entry:
  %sub = add i32 %num, -120
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %sub, 3
  %cond = select i1 %cmp, i32 %sub, i32 4
  switch i32 %cond, label %sw.default [
    i32 0, label %sw.bb
    i32 1, label %sw.bb2
    i32 2, label %sw.bb4

sw.bb:                                            ; preds = %entry
  %call = call i32 @call0()
  br label %cleanup

sw.bb2:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %call3 = call i32 @call1()
  br label %cleanup

sw.bb4:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %call5 = call i32 @call2()
  br label %cleanup

  %call6 = call i32 @call3()
  br label %cleanup

cleanup:                                          ; preds = %sw.default, %sw.bb4, %sw.bb2, %sw.bb
  %retval.0 = phi i32 [ %call6, %sw.default ], [ %call5, %sw.bb4 ], [ %call3, %sw.bb2 ], [ %call, %sw.bb ]
  ret i32 %retval.0
}
